basics.name: Roger Han
basics.label: Lead Mobile Engineer
basics.summary: Mobile engineer with 10+ years of native iOS experience building, shipping, and maintaining production apps in Swift, SwiftUI, UIKit, async/await, and MVVM-Coordinator architecture. Strong fit for Lead Mobile Engineer roles that need deep iOS judgement inside a modern React Native or cross-platform codebase: native integration, app architecture, performance, release quality, mentoring, and product-focused delivery. Recently focused on AI-assisted mobile development — building apps from scratch to store release across iOS and Android with Expo/React Native and Flutter, and designing the specs, reusable agent skills, and review workflows that keep AI-assisted code readable, tested, and production-quality.

work.name: Fiserv
work.summary: Lead mobile engineer for native iOS feature delivery and the team's AI-assisted development workflow.
highlights: Lead technical design for new mobile features, translating product requirements into maintainable iOS architecture and delivery plans.
Build and review native iOS features with Swift, SwiftUI, async/await, UIKit, and MVVM-Coordinator patterns.
Drive release readiness through risk analysis for new iOS, Xcode, and SDK versions before rollout.
Mentor engineers through design reviews, code reviews, and pairing, raising quality while keeping delivery moving.
Own features end to end — architecture and implementation through release, production support, and follow-up fixes.
Main contributor to the team's Speckit-based agentic development workflow: authored specs, reusable skills, and developer guidance that made AI-assisted output reviewable and trustworthy.
Turned ad-hoc AI prompting into explicit specs, implementation steps, and review expectations so the team can ship AI-assisted changes to production with stronger confidence.

work.name: Fiserv
highlights: Delivered production iOS applications from feature development through release and iteration.
Implemented mobile UI, networking, state management, authentication flows, and backend integrations.
Used fastlane and related CI/CD tooling to reduce manual release work and make signing/build steps more reliable.
Collaborated with product, design, backend, QA, and support to ship under tight deadlines while keeping quality visible.
Supported production issues and release troubleshooting across the mobile app lifecycle.

work.name: Fiserv
work.summary: Progressed from Junior (Sep 2014 – Jun 2016) to Intermediate Software Developer (Jun 2016 – Sep 2018), building and shipping iOS features across the mobile app lifecycle.

work.name: Zeald
highlights: Built responsive frontend web interfaces and integrations as part of a digital agency delivery team.

skills.name: Cross-platform mobile
keywords: Expo / React Native (Hermes, native modules, bridging to Swift)

skills.name: Mobile delivery / CI-CD
keywords: App Store Connect & Google Play submission

projects.name: Appifex — AI App Builder (live on the App Store)
projects.summary: Production React Native app that turns a plain-English prompt into a working full-stack app with live preview, shareable links, and source export. Built and shipped solo: integrated RevenueCat and Stripe for in-app purchases/subscriptions and Clerk OAuth for authentication, implemented home-screen widgets, and carried the app from scratch through App Store review and production deploy (App Store / Vercel).
highlights: Plain-English prompt to working full-stack app with live preview and source export
RevenueCat + Stripe IAP/subscriptions and Clerk OAuth
Solo: scratch through App Store review to released product

projects.name: appifex-dtc — Design-to-App CLI (open source)
projects.summary: CLI that generates compilable, linted native mobile apps from design files (Pencil / Figma / Stitch) — "design to TestFlight in one command." Encodes native best practices for both platforms (SwiftUI on iOS, Kotlin Compose on Android) via design tokenization, platform spec generation, test scaffolding, LLM code generation, compile/validate, and auto-fix loops. Added validation gating (Maestro UI tests, semgrep scans) and checkpoint-based resume (SQLite) so interrupted runs continue without restarting. Pipeline stages exposed as MCP tools for AI-agent orchestration.
highlights: Design files to compilable native apps in one command
Maestro + semgrep validation gating; SQLite checkpoint resume
Pipeline stages exposed as MCP tools

projects.name: Cross-platform AI work — Flutter & React Native
projects.summary: Shipped AI features across Flutter and Expo/React Native alongside native iOS, building scratch-to-store on both iOS (App Store) and Android (Google Play). Used MCP-connected E2E testing workflows to make AI-assisted changes safer and easier to verify.
